name: Stuffed Goose Breast with Caraway and Apples
recipeIngredient: 1 Large double goose breast, removed from bone
3 Green apples, peeled, cored, diced into 1-inch pieces
2 Boiled potatoes, peeled, diced into 1/4-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Lay the breast out in a brownie pan.
2. In a mixing bowl, stir together juniper, rosemary, olive oil and vinegar. Pour over the breast. Cover and refrigerate for 24 hours.
3. In a 14-inch saute pan, heat remaining oil over medium heat and add onions. Cook until softened, about 8 to 10 minutes.
4. Add apples, caraway and cooked potatoes and cook another 10 minutes, or until apples have started to soften. Remove from heat and allow to cool.
5. Add bread crumbs, parsley, cloves and egg. Season with salt and pepper. Set aside.
6. Preheat oven to 425 degrees F (220 degrees C).
7. Remove goose from marinade, brush off any excess, and pat dry. Season with salt and pepper and lay flat on a cutting board.
8. Lay the stuffing out evenly over the goose and roll up tightly like a jellyroll. Tie securely with butcher's twine and place in a roasting pan.
9. Roast in the oven for 45 to 50 minutes, or until internal temperature reaches 150 degrees F (65 degrees C). Remove, allow to rest 10 minutes, and carve. Serve with spiced white cabbage.
Yield: 4 servings
